     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import (
     4    "os"
     5    "path/filepath"
     6    "sort"
     7    "strings"
     8    "time"
     9  )
    10
    11  // HistorySource scans Pair draft/log sidecars under the data directory.
    12  type HistorySource struct {
    13    DataDir       string
    14    LegacyDataDir string
    15  }
    16
    17  func (s HistorySource) Scan(base string, cutoff time.Time) ([]HistoricalTag, error) {
    18    latest := map[string]time.Time{}
    19    for _, pattern := range []string{"draft-*.md", "log-*.md"} {
    20      matches, err := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(s.DataDir, pattern))
    21      if err != nil {
    22        return nil, err
    23      }
    24      for _, path := range matches {
    25        tag, ok := tagFromSidecar(filepath.Base(path))
    26        if !ok {
    27          continue
    28        }
    29        info, err := os.Stat(path)
    30        if err != nil {
    31          continue
    32        }
    33        if info.ModTime().Before(cutoff) {
    34          continue
    35        }
    36        if info.ModTime().After(latest[tag]) {
    37          latest[tag] = info.ModTime()
    38        }
    39      }
    40    }
    41    tags := make([]string, 0, len(latest))
    42    for tag := range latest {
    43      tags = append(tags, tag)
    44    }
    45    sort.Strings(tags)
    46    out := make([]HistoricalTag, 0, len(tags))
    47    for _, tag := range tags {
    48      out = append(out, HistoricalTag{Tag: tag, MTime: latest[tag], QueueCount: s.queueCount(tag)})
    49    }
    50    if s.LegacyDataDir != "" && s.LegacyDataDir != s.DataDir {
    51      legacy, err := s.scanLegacy(base, cutoff, latest)
    52      if err != nil {
    53        return nil, err
    54      }
    55      out = append(out, legacy...)
    56      sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].Tag < out[j].Tag })
    57    }
    58    return out, nil
    59  }
    60
    61  func (s HistorySource) scanLegacy(base string, cutoff time.Time, scoped map[string]time.Time) ([]HistoricalTag, error) {
    62    latest := map[string]time.Time{}
    63    for _, pattern := range []string{"draft-*.md", "log-*.md"} {
    64      matches, err := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(s.LegacyDataDir, pattern))
    65      if err != nil {
    66        return nil, err
    67      }
    68      for _, path := range matches {
    69        tag, ok := tagFromSidecar(filepath.Base(path))
    70        if !ok || !matchesHistoryBase(tag, base) {
    71          continue
    72        }
    73        if _, exists := scoped[tag]; exists {
    74          continue
    75        }
    76        info, err := os.Stat(path)
    77        if err != nil || info.ModTime().Before(cutoff) {
    78          continue
    79        }
    80        if info.ModTime().After(latest[tag]) {
    81          latest[tag] = info.ModTime()
    82        }
    83      }
    84    }
    85    tags := make([]string, 0, len(latest))
    86    for tag := range latest {
    87      tags = append(tags, tag)
    88    }
    89    sort.Strings(tags)
    90    out := make([]HistoricalTag, 0, len(tags))
    91    for _, tag := range tags {
    92      out = append(out, HistoricalTag{Tag: tag, MTime: latest[tag], QueueCount: s.legacyQueueCount(tag), LegacyUnscoped: true})
    93    }
    94    return out, nil
    95  }
    96
    97  // queueCount counts the queued prompts nvim parked under queue-<tag>/ — each is a
    98  // <digits>.md file (queue_count_for, shell 1335). Surfaced as the picker's amber
    99  // badge so a forgotten queue is visible before resuming.
   100  func (s HistorySource) queueCount(tag string) int {
   101    matches, err := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(s.DataDir, "queue-"+tag, "[0-9]*.md"))
   102    if err != nil {
   103      return 0
   104    }
   105    return len(matches)
   106  }
   107
   108  func (s HistorySource) legacyQueueCount(tag string) int {
   109    matches, err := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(s.LegacyDataDir, "queue-"+tag, "[0-9]*.md"))
   110    if err != nil {
   111      return 0
   112    }
   113    return len(matches)
   114  }
   115
   116  func tagFromSidecar(name string) (string, bool) {
   117    switch {
   118    case strings.HasPrefix(name, "draft-") && strings.HasSuffix(name, ".md"):
   119      return strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(name, "draft-"), ".md"), true
   120    case strings.HasPrefix(name, "log-") && strings.HasSuffix(name, ".md"):
   121      return strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(name, "log-"), ".md"), true
   122    default:
   123      return "", false
   124    }
   125  }
   126
   127  func matchesHistoryBase(tag, base string) bool {
   128    return tag == base || strings.HasPrefix(tag, base+"-")
   129  }

     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import (
     4    "encoding/json"
     5    "fmt"
     6    "strings"
     7  )
     8
     9  const minSessionComponentRunes = 4
    10
    11  type SessionNameEntry struct {
    12    SessionName string
    13    ScopeKey    string
    14    RepoRoot    string
    15    RepoName    string
    16    Tag         string
    17  }
    18
    19  type SessionNameIndex struct {
    20    Entries []SessionNameEntry
    21  }
    22
    23  func BuildSessionNameIndexLine(entry SessionNameEntry) (string, error) {
    24    data, err := json.Marshal(entry)
    25    if err != nil {
    26      return "", err
    27    }
    28    return string(data), nil
    29  }
    30
    31  func ParseSessionNameIndex(raw string) SessionNameIndex {
    32    var index SessionNameIndex
    33    for _, line := range strings.Split(raw, "\n") {
    34      line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
    35      if line == "" {
    36        continue
    37      }
    38      var entry SessionNameEntry
    39      if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry); err != nil {
    40        continue
    41      }
    42      index.Entries = append(index.Entries, entry)
    43    }
    44    return index
    45  }
    46
    47  type SessionNameExhausted struct {
    48    RepoName string
    49    Tag      string
    50  }
    51
    52  func (e SessionNameExhausted) Error() string {
    53    return fmt.Sprintf("repo/tag too long for zellij socket path; choose a shorter tag: %s/%s", e.RepoName, e.Tag)
    54  }
    55
    56  func PublicSessionBase(scope RepoScope, tag string) string {
    57    return "pair-" + NormalizeDisplayComponent(scope.DisplayName) + "-" + NormalizeDisplayComponent(tag)
    58  }
    59
    60  func BuildSessionNameCandidates(scope RepoScope, tag string, suffix int) []string {
    61    repo := []rune(NormalizeDisplayComponent(scope.DisplayName))
    62    tagPart := []rune(NormalizeDisplayComponent(tag))
    63    var out []string
    64    seen := map[string]bool{}
    65    for {
    66      name := publicSessionName(repo, tagPart, suffix)
    67      if !seen[name] {
    68        out = append(out, name)
    69        seen[name] = true
    70      }
    71      if len(repo) <= minSessionComponentRunes && len(tagPart) <= minSessionComponentRunes {
    72        break
    73      }
    74      if len(repo) >= len(tagPart) && len(repo) > minSessionComponentRunes {
    75        repo = repo[:len(repo)-1]
    76        continue
    77      }
    78      if len(tagPart) > minSessionComponentRunes {
    79        tagPart = tagPart[:len(tagPart)-1]
    80        continue
    81      }
    82    }
    83    return out
    84  }
    85
    86  func publicSessionName(repo, tag []rune, suffix int) string {
    87    name := "pair-" + string(repo) + "-" + string(tag)
    88    if suffix > 1 {
    89      name += fmt.Sprintf("-%d", suffix)
    90    }
    91    return name
    92  }
    93
    94  func AssignSessionName(index SessionNameIndex, live []Session, scope RepoScope, tag string, accepts func(string) bool) (string, SessionNameIndex, error) {
    95    if accepts == nil {
    96      accepts = func(string) bool { return true }
    97    }
    98    if prior, ok := index.latestFor(scope.Key, tag); ok && accepts(prior.SessionName) {
    99      return prior.SessionName, index, nil
   100    }
   101    for suffix := 1; suffix <= 100; suffix++ {
   102      for _, candidate := range BuildSessionNameCandidates(scope, tag, suffix) {
   103        if !accepts(candidate) || index.ownedByOther(candidate, scope.Key, tag) || liveOwnedByOther(candidate, live, index, scope.Key, tag) {
   104          continue
   105        }
   106        entry := SessionNameEntry{
   107          SessionName: candidate,
   108          ScopeKey:    scope.Key,
   109          RepoRoot:    scope.Root,
   110          RepoName:    scope.DisplayName,
   111          Tag:         tag,
   112        }
   113        index.Entries = append(index.Entries, entry)
   114        return candidate, index, nil
   115      }
   116    }
   117    return "", index, SessionNameExhausted{RepoName: scope.DisplayName, Tag: tag}
   118  }
   119
   120  func (i SessionNameIndex) latestFor(scopeKey, tag string) (SessionNameEntry, bool) {
   121    for n := len(i.Entries) - 1; n >= 0; n-- {
   122      e := i.Entries[n]
   123      if e.ScopeKey == scopeKey && e.Tag == tag {
   124        return e, true
   125      }
   126    }
   127    return SessionNameEntry{}, false
   128  }
   129
   130  func (i SessionNameIndex) ownerOf(sessionName string) (SessionNameEntry, bool) {
   131    for n := len(i.Entries) - 1; n >= 0; n-- {
   132      e := i.Entries[n]
   133      if e.SessionName == sessionName {
   134        return e, true
   135      }
   136    }
   137    return SessionNameEntry{}, false
   138  }
   139
   140  func (i SessionNameIndex) ownedByOther(sessionName, scopeKey, tag string) bool {
   141    e, ok := i.ownerOf(sessionName)
   142    return ok && (e.ScopeKey != scopeKey || e.Tag != tag)
   143  }
   144
   145  func liveOwnedByOther(sessionName string, live []Session, index SessionNameIndex, scopeKey, tag string) bool {
   146    liveHere := false
   147    for _, s := range live {
   148      if s.Name == sessionName && s.State != SessionExited {
   149        liveHere = true
   150        break
   151      }
   152    }
   153    if !liveHere {
   154      return false
   155    }
   156    e, ok := index.ownerOf(sessionName)
   157    return !ok || e.ScopeKey != scopeKey || e.Tag != tag
   158  }
   159
   160  func SessionsForScope(sessions []Session, index SessionNameIndex, scope RepoScope) []Session {
   161    var out []Session
   162    for _, session := range sessions {
   163      entry, ok := index.ownerOf(session.Name)
   164      if !ok || entry.ScopeKey != scope.Key {
   165        continue
   166      }
   167      session.Tag = entry.Tag
   168      session.RepoName = entry.RepoName
   169      out = append(out, session)
   170    }
   171    return out
   172  }

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import "path/filepath"
     4
     5  type legacyImportPair struct {
     6    src string
     7    dst string
     8  }
     9
    10  func legacyImportPlan(tag, globalDataDir, scopedDataDir string, exists func(string) bool) []legacyImportPair {
    11    var pairs []legacyImportPair
    12    for _, src := range renamePathsFor(tag, globalDataDir) {
    13      if !exists(src) {
    14        continue
    15      }
    16      rel, err := filepath.Rel(globalDataDir, src)
    17      if err != nil {
    18        continue
    19      }
    20      dst := filepath.Join(scopedDataDir, rel)
    21      if exists(dst) {
    22        continue
    23      }
    24      pairs = append(pairs, legacyImportPair{src: src, dst: dst})
    25    }
    26    return pairs
    27  }
    28
    29  func importLegacyFlatTag(rt Runtime, tag, globalDataDir, scopedDataDir string) bool {
    30    if globalDataDir == "" || scopedDataDir == "" || globalDataDir == scopedDataDir {
    31      return false
    32    }
    33    imported := false
    34    for _, pair := range legacyImportPlan(tag, globalDataDir, scopedDataDir, func(p string) bool {
    35      _, ok := rt.FileSize(p)
    36      return ok
    37    }) {
    38      if copyLegacyPath(rt, pair.src, pair.dst) {
    39        imported = true
    40      }
    41    }
    42    if copyLegacyPath(rt, filepath.Join(globalDataDir, "queue-"+tag), filepath.Join(scopedDataDir, "queue-"+tag)) {
    43      imported = true
    44    }
    45    return imported
    46  }
    47
    48  func copyLegacyPath(rt Runtime, src, dst string) bool {
    49    if raw, err := rt.ReadFile(src); err == nil {
    50      return rt.WriteAtomic(dst, raw) == nil
    51    }
    52    entries, err := rt.ReadDir(src)
    53    if err != nil {
    54      return false
    55    }
    56    imported := false
    57    for _, rel := range entries {
    58      raw, err := rt.ReadFile(filepath.Join(src, rel))
    59      if err != nil {
    60        continue
    61      }
    62      if err := rt.WriteAtomic(filepath.Join(dst, rel), raw); err == nil {
    63        imported = true
    64      }
    65    }
    66    return imported
    67  }

codex
I found one high-risk path: prompted creation can change the tag after session-name assignment. I’m checking whether that writes a mismatched public session name or falls back to old `pair-<tag>` naming, because that would break same-tag multi-repo isolation for the default bare startup.

     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import "strconv"
     4
     5  // LaunchAction is the guarded prototype's next launcher action.
     6  type LaunchAction string
     7
     8  const (
     9    ActionAttach LaunchAction = "attach"
    10    ActionCreate LaunchAction = "create"
    11    ActionPick   LaunchAction = "pick"
    12  )
    13
    14  // LaunchDecision is a pure create/attach/pick decision. Tag is canonical bare
    15  // form; SessionName is derived as pair-<tag> when a zellij session is named.
    16  type LaunchDecision struct {
    17    Action       LaunchAction
    18    Tag          string
    19    SessionName  string
    20    PromptName   bool
    21    LegacyImport bool
    22  }
    23
    24  // DecideLaunch decides the launch action without touching zellij, fzf, or disk.
    25  func DecideLaunch(args LaunchArgs, snap SessionSnapshot) (LaunchDecision, error) {
    26    if args.SelectedTag != "" {
    27      return createDecision(args.SelectedTag, sessionNameForTag(snap, args.SelectedTag), false), nil
    28    }
    29    if args.ForcedTag != "" {
    30      name := sessionNameForTag(snap, args.ForcedTag)
    31      if sessionBlocksReuse(snap, name) {
    32        return LaunchDecision{Action: ActionAttach, Tag: args.ForcedTag, SessionName: name}, nil
    33      }
    34      return createDecision(args.ForcedTag, name, false), nil
    35    }
    36    if hasDetached(snap) || len(snap.Historical) > 0 {
    37      return LaunchDecision{Action: ActionPick}, nil
    38    }
    39    tag := snap.BaseTag
    40    if tag == "" {
    41      tag = "pair"
    42    }
    43    tag = nextFreeTag(tag, snap)
    44    return createDecision(tag, sessionNameForTag(snap, tag), true), nil
    45  }
    46
    47  func createDecision(tag, session string, prompt bool) LaunchDecision {
    48    return LaunchDecision{Action: ActionCreate, Tag: tag, SessionName: session, PromptName: prompt}
    49  }
    50
    51  func sessionName(tag string) string {
    52    return "pair-" + tag
    53  }
    54
    55  func sessionNameForTag(snap SessionSnapshot, tag string) string {
    56    if snap.SessionNames != nil {
    57      if name := snap.SessionNames[tag]; name != "" {
    58        return name
    59      }
    60    }
    61    return sessionName(tag)
    62  }
    63
    64  func hasDetached(snap SessionSnapshot) bool {
    65    for _, sess := range snap.Sessions {
    66      if sess.State == SessionDetached {
    67        return true
    68      }
    69    }
    70    return false
    71  }
    72
    73  func sessionBlocksReuse(snap SessionSnapshot, name string) bool {
    74    for _, sess := range snap.Sessions {
    75      if sess.Name != name {
    76        continue
    77      }
    78      return sess.State == SessionAttached || sess.State == SessionDetached
    79    }
    80    return false
    81  }
    82
    83  func nextFreeTag(base string, snap SessionSnapshot) string {
    84    for i := 1; i <= 100; i++ {
    85      tag := base
    86      if i > 1 {
    87        tag = base + "-" + strconv.Itoa(i)
    88      }
    89      if !sessionBlocksReuse(snap, sessionNameForTag(snap, tag)) && !isHistorical(snap, tag) {
    90        return tag
    91      }
    92    }
    93    return base
    94  }
    95
    96  func isHistorical(snap SessionSnapshot, tag string) bool {
    97    for _, hist := range snap.Historical {
    98      if hist.Tag == tag {
    99        return true
   100      }
   101    }
   102    return false
   103  }

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import (
     4    "fmt"
     5    "strings"
     6  )
     7
     8  // The fzf session picker (#99 M5a, ported from bin/pair-shell 1428-1508). When a
     9  // bare `pair` finds detached and/or historical pair-<tag> sessions, fzf offers
    10  // them plus a "+ new" row. The row *build* is a pure function over the decision
    11  // snapshot (detached-first ordering, age grey-grading, the queued badge); only
    12  // the fzf call itself (resolvePick) is a Runtime effect. Picking an existing tag
    13  // is resume-by-name — it re-enters DecideLaunch under a ForcedTag, so attach-vs-
    14  // create + agent inference match `pair resume <tag>`.
    15
    16  // pickSelection is what a chosen picker row resolves to: a specific tag, or the
    17  // "+ new" row (create a fresh free-slot tag with the name prompt).
    18  type pickSelection struct {
    19    tag    string
    20    isNew  bool
    21    legacy bool
    22  }
    23
    24  const (
    25    ansiGreen = "\033[32m"
    26    ansiReset = "\033[0m"
    27    ansiAmber = "\033[38;5;214m" // the queued badge (xterm 214)
    28  )
    29
    30  // buildPickRows renders the picker's display rows (ANSI-colored for fzf --ansi)
    31  // and a map from each row's PLAIN text (what fzf --ansi returns, color stripped)
    32  // to its selection. Order mirrors the shell: live detached sessions (green)
    33  // first, then historical "no live session" rows (age-graded grey + amber queued
    34  // badge), then the "+ new <base> session" row. Historical tags that still have a
    35  // live pair-<tag> (any state) are deduped out — they already show as their own
    36  // live row (shell 1387).
    37  func buildPickRows(snap SessionSnapshot, base string, nowEpoch int64) (display []string, byPlain map[string]pickSelection) {
    38    byPlain = map[string]pickSelection{}
    39    live := map[string]bool{}
    40    for _, s := range snap.Sessions {
    41      live[sessionTag(s)] = true
    42    }
    43
    44    add := func(plain, colored string, sel pickSelection) {
    45      display = append(display, colored)
    46      byPlain[plain] = sel
    47    }
    48
    49    for _, s := range snap.Sessions {
    50      if s.State != SessionDetached {
    51        continue
    52      }
    53      plain := livePickLabel(s)
    54      add(plain, ansiGreen+plain+ansiReset, pickSelection{tag: sessionTag(s)})
    55    }
    56
    57    for _, h := range snap.Historical {
    58      if live[h.Tag] {
    59        continue // already surfaced as a live row
    60      }
    61      if h.LegacyUnscoped {
    62        plain := fmt.Sprintf("legacy unscoped %s  (manual import)", h.Tag)
    63        add(plain, AgeColor(int((nowEpoch-h.MTime.Unix())/secondsPerDay))+plain+ansiReset, pickSelection{tag: h.Tag, legacy: true})
    64        continue
    65      }
    66      baseRow := historicalPickLabel(h, nowEpoch)
    67      badgePlain, badgeColored := "", ""
    68      if h.QueueCount > 0 {
    69        badgePlain = fmt.Sprintf("   [⏎ %d queued]", h.QueueCount)
    70        badgeColored = fmt.Sprintf("   %s[⏎ %d queued]%s", ansiAmber, h.QueueCount, ansiReset)
    71      }
    72      days := int((nowEpoch - h.MTime.Unix()) / secondsPerDay)
    73      add(baseRow+badgePlain, AgeColor(days)+baseRow+ansiReset+badgeColored, pickSelection{tag: h.Tag})
    74    }
    75
    76    newLabel := fmt.Sprintf("+ new %s session", base)
    77    add(newLabel, newLabel, pickSelection{isNew: true})
    78    return display, byPlain
    79  }
    80
    81  func sessionTag(s Session) string {
    82    if s.Tag != "" {
    83      return s.Tag
    84    }
    85    return strings.TrimPrefix(s.Name, "pair-")
    86  }
    87
    88  func livePickLabel(s Session) string {
    89    if s.RepoName != "" || s.Agent != "" {
    90      agent := s.Agent
    91      if agent == "" {
    92        agent = "?"
    93      }
    94      repo := s.RepoName
    95      if repo == "" {
    96        repo = "?"
    97      }
    98      return fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s  %s  (detached)", repo, sessionTag(s), agent)
    99    }
   100    return s.Name
   101  }
   102
   103  func historicalPickLabel(h HistoricalTag, nowEpoch int64) string {
   104    age := FormatAge(nowEpoch, h.MTime.Unix())
   105    if h.RepoName != "" || h.Agent != "" {
   106      agent := h.Agent
   107      if agent == "" {
   108        agent = "?"
   109      }
   110      repo := h.RepoName
   111      if repo == "" {
   112        repo = "?"
   113      }
   114      return fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s  %s  (%s, no live session)", repo, h.Tag, agent, age)
   115    }
   116    return fmt.Sprintf("pair-%s  (%s, no live session)", h.Tag, age)
   117  }
   118
   119  // resolvePick presents the picker and maps the choice into a concrete launch
   120  // decision. aborted=true means the user dismissed fzf (ESC/empty) — the caller
   121  // exits 0. "+ new" builds a fresh free-slot create (with the name prompt); an
   122  // existing tag re-enters DecideLaunch under a ForcedTag (attach if live, else
   123  // create-by-name), the resume-by-name path.
   124  func resolvePick(rt Runtime, snap SessionSnapshot, base string, nowEpoch int64) (LaunchDecision, bool) {
   125    display, byPlain := buildPickRows(snap, base, nowEpoch)
   126    picked := rt.PickFromList("pick a pair session", display, 10)
   127    if picked == "" {
   128      return LaunchDecision{}, true
   129    }
   130    sel, ok := byPlain[picked]
   131    if !ok {
   132      return LaunchDecision{}, true // fzf returned an unmapped line — abort safely.
   133    }
   134    if sel.isNew {
   135      tag := nextFreeTag(base, snap)
   136      return createDecision(tag, sessionNameForTag(snap, tag), true), false
   137    }
   138    d, _ := DecideLaunch(LaunchArgs{ForcedTag: sel.tag}, snap) // never errors (no pick recursion)
   139    d.LegacyImport = sel.legacy
   140    return d, false
   141  }

 succeeded in 0ms:
   185  pair resume <tag>                # restart a tag with its saved config (native session)
   186  pair continue                    # list saved continuations (durable session handoffs)
   187  pair continue <slug> [agent]     # new session seeded from a continuation doc; prompts
   188                                   # for the tag, and forwards -- <args> to the agent
   189                                   # e.g. pair continue port claude -- --dangerously-skip-permissions
   190  pair [<agent>] -- <args...>      # forward args to agent on create
   191                                   # e.g. pair claude -- --resume
   192                                   #      pair -- --dangerously-skip-permissions
   193                                   #      pair codex -- -p "say hi"
   194  pair list                        # list pair-* sessions and attach state
   195  pair rename <old> <new>          # rename every tag-scoped file in
   196                                   # $PAIR_DATA_DIR from <old> to <new>;
   197                                   # refuses if pair-<old> is still tracked
   198                                   # by zellij (use Ctrl+Alt+n's (R) inside
   199                                   # a session for the live equivalent)
   200  pair -h, --help                  # show full help
   201  ```
   202
   203  The installed `pair` command is Go-owned. As of the #99 M5 port it runs the
   204  **native Go launcher in-process for every flow** — create, attach, quit, Alt+n /
   205  Shift+Alt+N restart, the `fzf` session picker, `list`/`rename`/`continue`,
   206  in-session compaction, and `--help` all happen in the Go binary. The legacy
   207  shell launcher `bin/pair-shell` has been **retired** (removed) — there is no
   208  shell fallback. Source and Homebrew installs use their adjacent asset roots. A copied
   209  standalone `pair` binary with no adjacent
   210  or build-time source root extracts its embedded Pair-owned runtime assets to
   211  `${PAIR_DATA_DIR:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/pair}/runtime/<digest>/pair-home`
   212  and runs with `PAIR_HOME` pointed there. External tools such as `zellij`,
   213  `nvim`, `fzf`, `jq`, clipboard tools, and agent CLIs are still installed
   214  separately.
   215
   216  `pair` is a **single binary** (built from `cmd/pair-go`): every helper is a
   217  `pair <subcommand>` (`pair wrap`, `pair scribe`, `pair review open`,
   218  `pair scrollback render`, `pair clip copy-on-select`, …), reached inside a
   219  session because the launcher fronts pair's own dir on the session PATH. In a dev
   220  shell sourced from `../ariadne/construct/dev-aliases.sh`, `pair` rebuilds from
   221  `cmd/pair-go` automatically before running.
   222
   223  Use `--` to separate pair's positional from agent flags. Without it, pair only takes `<agent>` as a positional and everything else is rejected.
   224
   225  Agent args (after `--`) are appended to the agent command line on **create**. Reattaching to an existing session does not re-launch the agent, so the args don't apply on attach. (The picker connects you to whatever's already running.)
   226
   227  **Hacking on pair?** Use `pair-dev` instead of `pair` — same arguments, but it rebuilds the `pair` binary from source (`make build`) on launch *and* on every Alt+n / Shift+Alt+N restart, so the zellij-spawned `pair wrap` always matches your working tree. (Deployed installs run `pair`, which uses the prebuilt binary and needs no Go toolchain.)
   228
   229  When `pair` runs and there's anything to pick — a detached `pair-*` session **or** a tag from this cwd used within the last 14 days — it shows an `fzf` picker. Detached rows come first, then historical rows annotated `(Nd ago, no live session)`, then a `+ new <agent> session` sentinel. A historical row whose session has prompts parked in its queue also carries an amber `[⏎ N queued]` badge, so you don't resume a session without remembering the work you queued up in it. Picking a historical row reuses the name and any surviving draft / saved agent config (same path as `pair resume <tag>`). Override the 14-day window with `PAIR_HISTORY_DAYS`; `PAIR_DEBUG_HISTORY=1 pair` prints the scan and exits without launching.
   230
   231  When the create flow runs, it prompts for the session name with the auto-suggested name as the default:
   232
   233  ```
   234  Session name [claude]: <Enter to accept, or type a custom name>
   235  ```
   236
   237  Custom names like `bugfix`, `blogging`, or `research` are allowed (chars: `A-Z a-z 0-9 - _`).
   238
   239  To detach mid-session: `Alt+d`. To re-attach: run `pair` again and pick from the list. To fully quit (no resurrect entry): `Alt+x`.
   240
   241  ## Resume a session by tag
   242
   243  Pair captures each new session's startup args plus the agent's own session id, keyed by tag. After `Alt+x` you'll see:
   244
   245  ```
   246  pair: saved session config for tag "pair-bugfix" (claude).
   247        resume with: pair resume pair-bugfix
   248  ```
   249
   250  Run that command and the picker + name prompt are skipped. Pair offers three options for what to do with the saved config:
   251
   252  ```
   253  saved config for tag 'bugfix' (claude)
   254    1) use saved params + session
   255         args=[--dangerously-skip-permissions]
   256         resume=8d745d08-4ecc-4474-969a-53c98a6fa5f0
   257    2) use saved params
   258         args=[--dangerously-skip-permissions]
   259         fresh session
   260    3) use new params + session            (only if you passed new args that differ)
   261         args=[<whatever you passed on this command>]
   262         resume=8d745d08-4ecc-4474-969a-53c98a6fa5f0
   263    4) use new params passed in            (only if you passed new args that differ)
   264         args=[<whatever you passed on this command>]
   265         fresh session
   266  ```
   267
   268  - **use saved params + session** replays the original launch args *and* points the agent at its previous session id (claude's `--resume`, codex's `resume <id>` subcommand, agy's `--conversation <id>`). Only shown if the agent's native session file is still on disk.
   269  - **use saved params** replays the args but starts a fresh agent session.
   270  - **use new params + session** swaps in the args you just passed on the command line, but keeps the prior session id. Only shown when both conditions hold: the native session file is on disk AND the new args differ from the saved ones (otherwise it would be byte-identical to row 1).
   271  - **use new params passed in** uses your new args with a fresh session. Only shown when the new args differ from the saved ones.
   272
   273  The agent (claude / codex / agy) is inferred from saved state, so `pair resume <tag>` is enough on its own — no need to repeat the agent positional. If `pair-<tag>` is still a running zellij session (e.g. you only `Alt+d` detached), `pair resume <tag>` re-attaches without prompting.
   274
   275  Saved configs live at `${XDG_DATA_HOME:-~/.local/share}/pair/config-<tag>-<agent>.json`.
   276
   277  ### `resume` vs `continue`
   278
   279  `pair resume` and `pair continue` restore two different *kinds* of state:
   280

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import (
     4    "io"
     5    "strings"
     6  )
     7
     8  // runRestart is the in-process port of bin/pair-restart.sh (#94 M1): resolve the
     9  // live session/tag/agent, write the restart marker (carrying new_session +
    10  // optional rename_to), touch the quit marker, then exec kill-session. It reuses
    11  // the exact Runtime seam runCompaction drives (compaction.go:54-68) — the effects
    12  // already live on OSRuntime, so nothing new is added to the seam. ExecKillSession
    13  // is terminal on the real runtime (syscall.Exec replaces the process), so the
    14  // return is reached only when the kill binary is missing or under the fake.
    15  func runRestart(rt Runtime, args LaunchArgs, session string, stderr io.Writer) int {
    16    if session == "" {
    17      _, _ = io.WriteString(stderr, "pair restart: ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME unset; cannot restart cleanly.\n")
    18      return 1
    19    }
    20    tag := strings.TrimPrefix(session, "pair-")
    21    rt.WriteRestartMarker(session, RestartMarker{
    22      Tag: tag,
    23      // InferAgent reads agent-<tag> (always present when the keybind fires —
    24      // cleanup removes it only AFTER the restart). Its config-<tag>-*.json
    25      // fallback is broader than the shell's plain `cat agent-<tag>`, but it
    26      // only ever fills an otherwise-empty agent=, never contradicts it —
    27      // a deliberate, safe divergence from the byte-faithful shell.
    28      Agent:      rt.InferAgent(tag),
    29      NewSession: args.NewSession,
    30      RenameTo:   args.RenameTo,
    31    })
    32    rt.TouchQuitMarker(session)
    33    rt.ExecKillSession(session)
    34    return 0
    35  }
    36
    37  // runQuit is the in-process port of bin/pair-quit.sh (#94 M1): touch the quit
    38  // marker so the outer loop's cleanup fires, then exec kill-session. No restart
    39  // marker — Alt+x is a full quit, not a reload.
    40  func runQuit(rt Runtime, session string, stderr io.Writer) int {
    41    if session == "" {
    42      _, _ = io.WriteString(stderr, "pair quit: ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME unset; cannot quit cleanly.\n")
    43      return 1
    44    }
    45    rt.TouchQuitMarker(session)
    46    rt.ExecKillSession(session)
    47    return 0
    48  }

     9
    10  // The `pair rename <old> <new>` offline subcommand + the inside-session rename
    11  // re-entry (#99 M5b, ported from bin/pair-shell 307-546). Moves every tag-scoped
    12  // sidecar from <old> to <new>. Enumeration is EXACT-name (never a glob — `rename
    13  // brain new` must not touch `*-brain-2-*` files, shell 315-319). The plan build is
    14  // pure over an injected `exists` predicate; only the mv + journal are effects.
    15
    16  // renameAgents is the hardcoded agent set whose per-(tag,agent) sidecars rename
    17  // carries (shell 408). A new agent must be added here (and nowhere else — the zip
    18  // design below needs it in one enumerator only).
    19  var renameAgents = []string{"claude", "codex", "agy"}
    20
    21  // renamePathsFor enumerates every candidate sidecar path for a tag, in a stable
    22  // order (shell rename_paths_for, 396-417). The order is identical for any tag, so
    23  // zip(renamePathsFor(old), renamePathsFor(new)) yields the (src,dst) pairing
    24  // directly — no base-name substitution needed (ARCH-PURE, drops shell 445-486).
    25  func renamePathsFor(tag, dataDir string) []string {
    26    var out []string
    27    for _, fam := range []string{
    28      "outer-tty", "pair-wrap-pid", "title-pid",
    29      "agent", "agent-pid", "agent-output", "agent-picks",
    30      "layout-mode", "queue", "quote", "image-capture",
    31    } {
    32      out = append(out, filepath.Join(dataDir, fam+"-"+tag))
    33    }
    34    out = append(out,
    35      filepath.Join(dataDir, "image-capture-"+tag+".done"),
    36      filepath.Join(dataDir, "draft-"+tag+".md"),
    37      filepath.Join(dataDir, "log-"+tag+".md"),
    38      filepath.Join(dataDir, "nvim-pid-"+tag+"-draft"),
    39      filepath.Join(dataDir, "nvim-pid-"+tag+"-scrollback"),
    40    )
    41    for _, a := range renameAgents {
    42      out = append(out,
    43        filepath.Join(dataDir, "config-"+tag+"-"+a+".json"),
    44        filepath.Join(dataDir, "pane-"+tag+"-"+a+".json"),
    45        filepath.Join(dataDir, "scrollback-"+tag+"-"+a+".ansi"),
    46        filepath.Join(dataDir, "scrollback-"+tag+"-"+a+".raw"),
    47        filepath.Join(dataDir, "scrollback-"+tag+"-"+a+".viewport"),
    48        filepath.Join(dataDir, "scrollback-"+tag+"-"+a+".events.jsonl"),
    49        filepath.Join(dataDir, "draft-"+tag+"-"+a+".md"),
    50      )
    51    }
    52    return out
    53  }
    54
    55  // renamePair is one src→dst move in the rename plan.
    56  type renamePair struct{ src, dst string }
    57
    58  // validateRenameTags normalizes both tags and applies rename's own gates: charset
    59  // (NormalizeTag), ≤256 length (shell 359-364), and old!=new (shell 365-368).
    60  func validateRenameTags(oldRaw, newRaw string) (old, new string, err error) {
    61    if old, err = NormalizeTag(oldRaw); err != nil {
    62      return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid tag: %w", err)
    63    }
    64    if new, err = NormalizeTag(newRaw); err != nil {
    65      return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid tag: %w", err)
    66    }
    67    for _, t := range []string{old, new} {
    68      if len(t) > 256 {
    69        return "", "", fmt.Errorf("tag '%s' is too long (max 256)", t)
    70      }
    71    }
    72    if old == new {
    73      return "", "", fmt.Errorf("old and new tag are the same ('%s')", old)
    74    }
    75    return old, new, nil
    76  }
    77
    78  // renamePlan builds the (src,dst) move list for old→new, pure over the injected
    79  // `exists` predicate (shell 419-501). Refuses if the new tag is occupied (any of
    80  // its sidecars already exist — a broader guard that subsumes the shell's per-pair
    81  // dst-exists check) or if no source files exist for old.
    82  func renamePlan(old, new, dataDir string, exists func(string) bool) ([]renamePair, error) {
    83    newPaths := renamePathsFor(new, dataDir)
    84    for _, p := range newPaths {
    85      if exists(p) {
    86        return nil, fmt.Errorf("tag '%s' is occupied — '%s' exists", new, p)
    87      }
    88    }
    89    oldPaths := renamePathsFor(old, dataDir)
    90    var pairs []renamePair
    91    for i, src := range oldPaths {
    92      if exists(src) {
    93        pairs = append(pairs, renamePair{src: src, dst: newPaths[i]})
    94      }
    95    }
    96    if len(pairs) == 0 {
    97      return nil, fmt.Errorf("no files found for tag '%s' in %s", old, dataDir)
    98    }
    99    return pairs, nil
   100  }
   101
   102  // renameJournal serializes the plan as `src\tdst` lines — written before the mv
   103  // loop so a crash leaves a forensic breadcrumb + drives the reverse-rollback
   104  // (shell 513-514/535-539).
   105  func renameJournal(pairs []renamePair) string {
   106    var b strings.Builder
   107    for _, p := range pairs {
   108      b.WriteString(p.src)
   109      b.WriteByte('\t')
   110      b.WriteString(p.dst)
   111      b.WriteByte('\n')
   112    }
   113    return b.String()
   114  }
   115
   116  // sessionTracked reports whether a session named `name` exists in ANY state
   117  // (attached / detached / EXITED) — rename's offline gate blocks on a resurrectable
   118  // row too, so it is membership over Sessions(), NOT SessionBlocksReuse (shell
   119  // 380-390 + the resurrectable-contract comment 821-823).
   120  func sessionTracked(sessions []Session, name string) bool {
   121    for _, s := range sessions {
   122      if s.Name == name {
   123        return true
   124      }
   125    }
   126    return false
   127  }
   128
   129  func sessionTrackedForTag(sessions []Session, index SessionNameIndex, scopeKey, tag string) bool {
   130    for _, entry := range index.Entries {
   131      if entry.Tag != tag {
   132        continue
   133      }
   134      if scopeKey != "" && entry.ScopeKey != scopeKey {
   135        continue
   136      }
   137      if sessionTracked(sessions, entry.SessionName) {
   138        return true
   139      }
   140    }
   141    return sessionTracked(sessions, "pair-"+tag)
   142  }
   143
   144  // runRename drives `pair rename [--restart-check] <old> <new>` (shell 307-546):
   145  // validate the tags, gate on tracked sessions, build the pure move plan, then
   146  // (unless --restart-check) journal + mv with reverse-rollback on failure. Returns
   147  // the exit code; the offline subcommand never launches zellij.
   148  func runRename(rt Runtime, args LaunchArgs, dataDir string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) int {
   149    return runRenameScoped(rt, args, dataDir, "", stdout, stderr)
   150  }
   151
   152  func runRenameScoped(rt Runtime, args LaunchArgs, dataDir, scopeKey string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) int {
   153    old, newTag, err := validateRenameTags(args.RenameOld, args.RenameNew)
   154    if err != nil {
   155      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair rename: %v\n", err)
   156      return 1
   157    }
   158    if _, ok := rt.FileSize(dataDir); !ok {
   159      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair rename: data dir not found: %s\n", dataDir)
   160      return 1
   161    }
   162
   163    // Session gate (shell 378-391): empty on zellij-absent/query-error, matching
   164    // the shell's `|| true` (gate skipped). --restart-check skips the live-old
   165    // gate (the real mv runs post-kill from the restart re-entry).
   166    sessions, _ := rt.Sessions()
   167    index, _ := rt.ReadSessionNameIndex()
   168    if !args.RenameCheckOnly && sessionTrackedForTag(sessions, index, scopeKey, old) {
   169      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair rename: session 'pair-%s' is still tracked by zellij.\n", old)
   170      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "             Quit it first (Alt+x), or use the in-session rename.\n")
   171      return 1
   172    }
   173    if sessionTrackedForTag(sessions, index, scopeKey, newTag) {
   174      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair rename: session 'pair-%s' already exists in zellij.\n", newTag)
   175      return 1
   176    }
   177
   178    pairs, err := renamePlan(old, newTag, dataDir, func(p string) bool { _, ok := rt.FileSize(p); return ok })
   179    if err != nil {
   180      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair rename: %v\n", err)
   181      return 1
   182    }
   183
   184    if args.RenameCheckOnly {
   185      fmt.Fprintf(stdout, "pair rename: ok (%d file(s) would move from '%s' to '%s')\n", len(pairs), old, newTag)
   186      return 0
   187    }
   188
   189    journal := filepath.Join(dataDir, ".rename-"+old+"-to-"+newTag+".journal")
   190    _ = rt.WriteAtomic(journal, renameJournal(pairs))
   191    fmt.Fprintf(stdout, "pair rename: %d file(s) %s → %s\n", len(pairs), old, newTag)
   192    done := 0
   193    for _, p := range pairs {
   194      if err := rt.Rename(p.src, p.dst); err != nil {
   195        fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair rename: mv failed: %s → %s: %v\n", p.src, p.dst, err)
   196        fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair rename: rolling back %d completed rename(s)...\n", done)
   197        for j := done - 1; j >= 0; j-- {
   198          _ = rt.Rename(pairs[j].dst, pairs[j].src) // reverse the completed moves
   199        }
   200        return 1 // keep the journal — diagnostic (shell 540)
   201      }
   202      done++
   203    }
   204    rt.Remove(journal)
   205    fmt.Fprintf(stdout, "pair rename: ok\n")
   206    return 0
   207  }

codex
There is another concrete bug in the in-session lifecycle path: `restart` still derives the tag by trimming `pair-` from the public zellij name. With scoped names that public name includes repo+tag, so restart metadata points at the wrong tag and wrong ledger/cache files.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba cmd/internal/launcher/createflow.go | sed -n '1,128p'" in /Users/xianxu/workspace/pair
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import (
     4    "fmt"
     5    "io"
     6    "os"
     7    "path/filepath"
     8    "strings"
     9    "time"
    10  )
    11
    12  // RunLaunch is the native launcher's in-process driver (#99 M2 create + M3
    13  // attach/restart/quit + M5b compaction/pick/rename/continue): a thin loop over the
    14  // pure deciders (DecideLaunch + createlogic.go + M1's agentargs) that maps each
    15  // shell orchestration step to a Runtime effect. Each iteration runs one create OR
    16  // attach handoff (blocking fork+wait, so control returns here), runs the Alt+x
    17  // quit cleanup, then — if a restart marker is present — re-decides under the
    18  // marker's tag/agent (applying any rename_to move + continue re-seed), replacing
    19  // the shell's `exec $0`. As of M5c the shell is retired — every launch path is
    20  // handled here (compact / reject-in-pane / pick / rename / continue all native);
    21  // user-facing messages are on the writer, the int is the exit code, the returned
    22  // error is always nil.
    23  func RunLaunch(opts LaunchOptions, rt Runtime, stderr io.Writer) (int, error) {
    24    env := normalizeEnv(opts.Env)
    25
    26    // Prepend the resolved asset root's bin/ to PATH so zellij (and its panes:
    27    // pair-wrap, copy_command "copy-on-select", Run "pair-help"/openers, and the
    28    // nvim viewers) resolve the bundled helpers by bare name. The retired shell
    29    // bin/pair did this; the Go launcher that replaced it (#99 M5c) dropped it, so
    30    // a copied/Homebrew install (bin/ not on the user's PATH) couldn't launch (#95).
    31    // RunLaunch is the sole zellij-spawning path (create/attach/resurrect/restart-
    32    // loop), so once here covers them all.
    33    exeDir := ""
    34    if exe, err := os.Executable(); err == nil {
    35      exeDir = filepath.Dir(exe)
    36    }
    37    rt.SetEnv("PATH", prependBinToPath(opts.PairHome, exeDir, os.Getenv("PATH")))
    38
    39    // #55 in-session compaction (M5b): `pair continue <slug>` from inside the
    40    // matching pane parks the scrollback (copy), drops a restart marker carrying
    41    // the slug, and kills the session — the outer loop below then re-launches
    42    // fresh, seeded from the slug. First entry only: a restart re-launch is the
    43    // same outer process, never in a pane.
    44    if opts.ContinueSlug != "" &&
    45      compactionDecision(opts.ForceInSession, rt.InZellijPane() || opts.FakeInZellij, opts.PairTag, opts.ZellijSession) {
    46      return runCompaction(opts, rt, stderr)
    47    }
    48    // Otherwise a launch from inside a pane can't proceed (a nested --session
    49    // would break; the create path's prompt would block) — shell 1064-1067.
    50    if rt.InZellijPane() {
    51      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair: already running inside a zellij session.\n")
    52      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "      detach first (Alt+d) or run pair from a fresh terminal.\n")
    53      return 1, nil
    54    }
    55
    56    // Startup nvim hygiene (shell 1243): reap embeds whose pair-<tag> session is
    57    // gone (an external kill / reboot leaves no quit marker). Once, up front — a
    58    // clean restart below leaves nothing new to sweep.
    59    if sessions, err := rt.Sessions(); err == nil {
    60      rt.SweepOrphanNvim(liveTagsForSweep(sessions))
    61    }
    62
    63    for {
    64      step, err := runOnce(opts, env, rt, stderr)
    65      if err != nil {
    66        return step.code, err // defensive: runOnce messages + returns nil now
    67      }
    68      if !step.handedOff {
    69        return step.code, nil // aborted or errored before the blocking handoff
    70      }
    71      runCleanup(env, rt, step, opts.ParkPromptTimeout, stderr)
    72
    73      m, ok := rt.TakeRestartMarker(step.session)
    74      if !ok {
    75        return step.code, nil // no restart pending — done.
    76      }
    77      rTag := firstNonEmpty(m.Tag, step.tag)
    78      rAgent := firstNonEmpty(m.Agent, step.agent)
    79
    80      // rename_to re-entry (M5b, shell 743-750): move the tag-scoped sidecars
    81      // old→new FIRST — the session was just killed, so the live-old gate passes
    82      // — then the config read + relaunch below run under the new tag. A failure
    83      // keeps the old tag (don't strand the user).
    84      if m.RenameTo != "" {
    85        if runRename(rt, LaunchArgs{RenameOld: rTag, RenameNew: m.RenameTo}, env.DataDir, io.Discard, stderr) == 0 {
    86          rTag = m.RenameTo
    87        } else {
    88          fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair: rename to '%s' failed; continuing under '%s'.\n", m.RenameTo, rTag)
    89        }
    90      }
    91
    92      configPath := resolveConfigPath(rt, env.DataDir, rTag, rAgent)
    93      plan := planRestart(m, rTag, rAgent, readSavedConfig(rt, configPath))
    94      if plan.DropConfig {
    95        rt.Remove(configPath) // Shift+Alt+N / compaction: drop the config so create mints fresh.
    96      }
    97      opts.Args = plan.Args
    98      opts.SkipConfigPicker = true
    99      // A #55 compaction re-entry re-seeds the draft from the continuation slug
   100      // (M5b); every other restart re-entry leaves the draft as-is. The re-entry
   101      // is the outer process (never in a pane), so ContinueSlug stays cleared —
   102      // it can't re-trigger compaction, only the ContinueDoc draft re-seed.
   103      opts.ContinueDoc = ""
   104      opts.ContinueSlug = ""
   105      if plan.ContinueSlug != "" {
   106        if docPath, _, ok := rt.ResolveContinuationDoc(plan.ContinueSlug); ok {
   107          opts.ContinueDoc = docPath
   108        }
   109      }
   110    }
   111  }
   112
   113  // launchStep is one iteration's outcome: the exit code, the session handed off
   114  // (so cleanup + the restart marker key off it), the resolved tag/agent (the
   115  // restart plan's current-run defaults), and whether the blocking handoff ran
   116  // (only then do cleanup + restart apply).
   117  type launchStep struct {
   118    code      int
   119    session   string
   120    tag       string
   121    agent     string
   122    handedOff bool
   123  }
   124
   125  // runOnce runs one decision → create-or-attach handoff. Every outcome is handled
   126  // natively (#99 M5c — no shell to fall back to): a handled abort/error returns
   127  // handedOff=false with the exit code already messaged on stderr.
   128  func runOnce(opts LaunchOptions, env Env, rt Runtime, stderr io.Writer) (launchStep, error) {

    15  // runAttach ports the shell's attach branch (1701-1723): re-attach to a live

    17  // refreshes the outer-tty/title/cmux/poller, but it re-uses the existing pane's
    18  // agent (no fresh spawn, no arg composition) and blocks on the attach handoff so
    19  // the loop regains control for cleanup + restart. agent is the inferred title
    20  // agent (the on-disk agent-<tag> record, resolved by the caller).
    21  func runAttach(opts LaunchOptions, env Env, rt Runtime, tag, session, agent string) (int, error) {

    23      session = "pair-" + tag
    24    }

    26    // before the branch; the attach branch itself only re-exports PAIR_TAG).

    32    _ = rt.Touch(filepath.Join(env.DataDir, "draft-"+tag+".md"))

    39    // matches the running pane's agent regardless of any bare-`pair` default.
    40    rt.SpawnTitlePoller(tag, agent)
    41
    42    return rt.AttachSession(session, filepath.Join(opts.PairHome, "zellij"))
    43  }
    44
    45  // runCleanup ports cleanup_quit_marker (shell 1520-1647): after a blocking
    46  // handoff returns, if the Alt+x quit marker is present, tear the session down —
    47  // delete the zellij record, reap nvim, offer to park the scrollback, remove the

    50  // Runs after BOTH create and attach handoffs (either can leave a quit marker).
    51  func runCleanup(env Env, rt Runtime, step launchStep, parkTimeout int, out io.Writer) {

    53      return

    57    // removed below, so the park path + resume hint name the right binary

    65    rt.DeleteSession(step.session)

    68    // Park-nudge (ariadne#91): the scrollback is the only on-disk record and

    88    for _, rel := range []string{

    90      "agent-" + step.tag,
    91      "agent-output-" + step.tag,
    92      "pair-wrap-pid-" + step.tag,
    93      "adapt-" + step.tag + ".jsonl",
    94      "image-capture-" + step.tag,
    95      "image-capture-" + step.tag + ".done",
    96      "pane-" + step.tag + "-" + quitAgent + ".json",

    99    }

   103      rt.Remove(sbBase + ".raw")

   134  // readSavedConfig loads config-<tag>-<agent>.json for the restart plan; a
   135  // missing/unusable file yields the zero savedConfig (no resume, no saved args).
   136  func readSavedConfig(rt Runtime, configPath string) savedConfig {
   137    raw, err := rt.ReadFile(configPath)
   138    if err != nil {
   139      return savedConfig{}
   140    }
   141    cfg, _ := parseConfig(raw)
   142    return cfg
   143  }

   145  // liveTagsForSweep projects the live pair session names to their bare tags for

   148  // the shell's all_pair, the full `list-sessions --short` list).
   149  func liveTagsForSweep(sessions []Session) []string {
   150    tags := make([]string, 0, len(sessions))
   151    for _, s := range sessions {
   152      tags = append(tags, strings.TrimPrefix(s.Name, "pair-"))
   153    }
   154    return tags
   155  }

   171        rest = rest[:i] // strip trailing -<agent> to recover <tag>
   172      }
   173      return rest
   174    }
   175    return ""
   176  }

     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import (
     4    "fmt"
     5    "io"
     6    "strings"
     7  )
     8
     9  // In-session compaction (#55, #99 M5b, ported from bin/pair-shell 1019-1062).
    10  // `pair continue <slug>` run from INSIDE the matching live pane must not
    11  // fresh-start (a nested --session would break, and the create path's name prompt
    12  // would block). Instead: park the scrollback (copy — pair-wrap is still appending
    13  // to .raw), drop a restart marker carrying the slug, kill the session; the outer
    14  // RunLaunch loop then re-launches fresh under the same tag. The decision + marker
    15  // serialization are pure; park/marker-write/kill are Runtime effects.
    16
    17  // compactionDecision decides whether an in-pane `continue` compacts (shell
    18  // 1035-1043). PAIR_FORCE_IN_SESSION forces it (bypassing both halves);
    19  // otherwise it needs the ancestry/fake half AND a tag-match — the guard against
    20  // cmux leaking ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME to sibling non-pair panes (env-only detection
    21  // would park+kill the wrong session).
    22  func compactionDecision(forceInSession, inPaneOrFake bool, pairTag, zellijSession string) bool {
    23    if forceInSession {
    24      return true
    25    }
    26    return inPaneOrFake && pairTag != "" && zellijSession == "pair-"+pairTag
    27  }
    28
    29  // serializeRestartMarker renders a RestartMarker as the `key=value` text
    30  // pair-restart.sh's format expects — the inverse of parseRestartMarker, so a
    31  // marker written here round-trips through TakeRestartMarker. Only non-empty
    32  // fields are emitted (matching the shell's compaction write, 1052-1057).
    33  func serializeRestartMarker(m RestartMarker) string {
    34    var b strings.Builder
    35    fmt.Fprintf(&b, "tag=%s\n", m.Tag)
    36    fmt.Fprintf(&b, "agent=%s\n", m.Agent)
    37    if m.NewSession {
    38      b.WriteString("new_session=1\n")
    39    }
    40    if m.RenameTo != "" {
    41      fmt.Fprintf(&b, "rename_to=%s\n", m.RenameTo)
    42    }
    43    if m.Continue != "" {
    44      fmt.Fprintf(&b, "continue=%s\n", m.Continue)
    45    }
    46    return b.String()
    47  }
    48
    49  // runCompaction executes the in-pane compaction (shell 1045-1060): park the
    50  // scrollback (copy), write the restart marker (new_session + continue slug),
    51  // touch the quit marker, then exec kill-session. ExecKillSession is terminal
    52  // (replaces the process), so the return is unreachable on the real runtime — it
    53  // exists so the fake-Runtime loop test can observe the sequence.
    54  func runCompaction(opts LaunchOptions, rt Runtime, stderr io.Writer) (int, error) {
    55    tag := opts.PairTag
    56    if tag == "" {
    57      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair: compaction needs PAIR_TAG\n") // shell 1046
    58      return 1, nil
    59    }
    60    agent := firstNonEmpty(opts.PairAgent, opts.Args.Agent, "claude")
    61    fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair: compacting pair-%s — parking scrollback, restarting from continuation…\n", tag)
    62    session := "pair-" + tag
    63    rt.ParkScrollback(tag, agent, false) // copy: the live .raw is still being appended
    64    rt.WriteRestartMarker(session, RestartMarker{Tag: tag, Agent: agent, NewSession: true, Continue: opts.ContinueSlug})
    65    rt.TouchQuitMarker(session)
    66    rt.ExecKillSession(session)
    67    return 0, nil
    68  }

codex
A third lifecycle issue surfaced: in-pane compaction still checks `ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME == "pair-"+PAIR_TAG` and writes/kills `pair-<tag>`. That cannot work with the new public session names, so `pair continue <slug>` from inside a scoped session will either refuse or target the wrong session.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1  package sessionwatch
     2
     3  import (
     4    "fmt"
     5    "io"
     6    "os"
     7    "time"
     8
     9    "github.com/xianxu/pair/cmd/internal/adapt"
    10  )
    11
    12  // RunCLI is the pair-session-watch command body, shared by the
    13  // bin/pair-session-watch shim and the `pair session-watch` dispatcher route. It
    14  // parses argv into Options and drives the watcher; getenv/stderr are injected so
    15  // it is testable, and it no-ops (exit 0) when required args are missing.
    16  func RunCLI(args []string, getenv func(string) string, stderr io.Writer) int {
    17    opts, ok := buildOptions(args, getenv)
    18    if !ok {
    19      return 0
    20    }
    21    cleanupPairTag := ensurePairTag(opts.Tag)
    22    defer cleanupPairTag()
    23    logger := adapt.Open("session-watch", opts.Agent)
    24    defer logger.Close()
    25    if err := Run(opts, NewOSRuntime(logger)); err != nil {
    26      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair-session-watch: %v\n", err)
    27      return 1
    28    }
    29    return 0
    30  }
    31
    32  func ensurePairTag(tag string) func() {
    33    if os.Getenv("PAIR_TAG") != "" || tag == "" {
    34      return func() {}
    35    }
    36    _ = os.Setenv("PAIR_TAG", tag)
    37    return func() { _ = os.Unsetenv("PAIR_TAG") }
    38  }
    39
    40  func buildOptions(args []string, getenv func(string) string) (Options, bool) {
    41    if len(args) < 3 {
    42      return Options{}, false
    43    }
    44    home := getenv("HOME")
    45    dataDir := getenv("PAIR_DATA_DIR")
    46    if dataDir == "" {
    47      dataDir = adapt.DataDir()
    48    }
    49    return Options{
    50      Agent:   args[0],
    51      Tag:     args[1],
    52      Cwd:     args[2],
    53      Args:    append([]string(nil), args[3:]...),
    54      Home:    home,
    55      DataDir: dataDir,
    56      PIDWait: ParseDurationSeconds(getenv("PAIR_SESSION_WATCH_PID_WAIT_SECONDS"), 2*time.Second),
    57      Timeout: 60 * time.Second,
    58      Poll:    100 * time.Millisecond,
    59    }, true
    60  }

    38
    39  // Run discovers the async agent session id and writes config-<tag>-<agent>.json.
    40  func Run(opts Options, rt Runtime) error {
    41    spec, ok := SpecForAgent(opts.Agent, opts.Home)
    42    if !ok || opts.Tag == "" || opts.DataDir == "" {
    43      return nil
    44    }
    45    if opts.PIDWait <= 0 {
    46      opts.PIDWait = 2 * time.Second
    47    }
    48    if opts.Timeout <= 0 {
    49      opts.Timeout = 60 * time.Second
    50    }
    51    if opts.Poll <= 0 {
    52      opts.Poll = 100 * time.Millisecond
    53    }
    54
    55    watchStart := rt.Now()
    56    pidFile := filepath.Join(opts.DataDir, "agent-pid-"+opts.Tag)
    57    out := filepath.Join(opts.DataDir, "config-"+opts.Tag+"-"+opts.Agent+".json")
    58
    59    pidDeadline := watchStart.Add(opts.PIDWait)
    60    for {
    61      if fresh, _ := freshPID(pidFile, watchStart, rt); fresh {
    62        break
    63      }
    64      if !rt.Now().Before(pidDeadline) {
    65        break
    66      }
    67      rt.Sleep(opts.Poll)
    68    }
    69
    70    rootPID := ""
    71    agentStart := time.Time{}
    72    if fresh, mod := freshPID(pidFile, watchStart, rt); fresh {
    73      if data, err := rt.ReadFile(pidFile); err == nil {
    74        rootPID = strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
    75        agentStart = mod
    76      }
    77    }
    78
    79    legacyExisting := map[string]bool{}
    80    if rootPID == "" {
    81      files, _ := rt.ListFiles(spec.WatchDir)
    82      for _, file := range files {
    83        legacyExisting[file] = true
    84      }
    85    }
    86
    87    nmLogged := false
    88    deadline := watchStart.Add(opts.Timeout)
    89    for rt.Now().Before(deadline) {
    90      if rootPID != "" && !rt.ProcessAlive(rootPID) {
    91        return nil
    92      }
    93
    94      result := discover(spec, rootPID, agentStart, legacyExisting, rt)
    95      if result.ID != "" {
    96        payload, err := ConfigJSON(ConfigPayload{
    97          Agent:     opts.Agent,
    98          Args:      StripResumeArgs(opts.Agent, opts.Args),
    99          SessionID: result.ID,
   100        })
   101        if err != nil {
   102          return err
   103        }
   104        if err := rt.AtomicWrite(out, payload); err != nil {
   105          return err
   106        }
   107        rt.Log(adapt.Fired, "session_id="+result.ID)
   108        return nil
   109      }
   110      if result.NearMiss && !nmLogged {
   111        rt.Log(adapt.NearMiss, "matched session file but no id extracted: "+filepath.Base(result.Path))
   112        nmLogged = true
   113      }
   114
   115      rt.Sleep(opts.Poll)
   116    }
   117
   118    rt.Log(adapt.Fail, "no session id within 60s deadline (agent="+opts.Agent+")")
   119    return nil
   120  }

   265
   266  func (OSRuntime) SetTerminalTitle(session string) {
   267    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, "\033]0;%s\007", session)
   268  }
   269
   270  // --- ProcOps ---------------------------------------------------------------
   271
   272  func (r OSRuntime) SpawnSessionWatcher(agent, tag, cwd string, agentArgs []string) {
   273    spawnDetached(sessionWatcherArgv(runningPairExe(r.PairHome), agent, tag, cwd, agentArgs), nil)
   274  }
   275
   276  func (r OSRuntime) SpawnTitlePoller(tag, agent string) {
   277    spawnDetached(titlePollerArgv(runningPairExe(r.PairHome), tag, agent), nil)
   278  }
   279
   280  // runningPairExe resolves the running `pair` executable for the self-exec
   281  // sidecar spawns (#104 M2 folded pair-title/pair-session-watch into `pair title`
   282  // / `pair session-watch`). The launcher is always `pair`, so os.Executable() is
   283  // the pair binary directly — this works in the copied/Homebrew layout where
   284  // $PAIR_HOME/bin holds no `pair` (pair is never in its own runtime bundle).
   285  // (cf. clipcmd.siblingPairExe, which may run under a helper's name and so
   286  // resolves the pair sibling instead.) Falls back to $PAIR_HOME/bin/pair only if
   287  // os.Executable() is unavailable.
   288  func runningPairExe(pairHome string) string {
   289    if exe, err := os.Executable(); err == nil && exe != "" {
   290      return exe
   291    }
   292    return filepath.Join(pairHome, "bin", "pair")

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1  package launcher
     2
     3  import (
     4    "fmt"
     5    "io"
     6    "strings"
     7  )
     8
     9  // The `pair list` / `ls` subcommand (#99 M5a, ported from bin/pair-shell 228-306).
    10  // A read-only listing of pair-<tag> zellij sessions with their resolved agent and
    11  // attach state. The row gather (zellij queries + agent resolution + client counts)
    12  // is a Runtime effect (ListSessions); the table + status-string rendering is pure.
    13
    14  // listStatus renders a row's STATUS column purely from its state + client count:
    15  // an exited resurrect record, a detached (0-client) live session, or an attached
    16  // session with its client count (shell 283-299).
    17  func listStatus(row ListRow) string {
    18    switch {
    19    case row.State == SessionExited:
    20      return "exited"
    21    case row.Clients <= 0:
    22      return "detached"
    23    case row.Clients == 1:
    24      return "attached (1 client)"
    25    default:
    26      return fmt.Sprintf("attached (%d clients)", row.Clients)
    27    }
    28  }
    29
    30  // formatListTable renders the SESSION/AGENT/STATUS table (or the empty-set line).
    31  // Pure over the gathered rows so the formatting is unit-tested directly.
    32  func formatListTable(rows []ListRow) string {
    33    if len(rows) == 0 {
    34      return "no pair sessions\n"
    35    }
    36    out := fmt.Sprintf("%-30s %-10s %s\n", "SESSION", "AGENT", "STATUS")
    37    for _, r := range rows {
    38      agent := r.Agent
    39      if agent == "" {
    40        agent = "?"
    41      }
    42      out += fmt.Sprintf("%-30s %-10s %s\n", r.Session, agent, listStatus(r))
    43    }
    44    return out
    45  }
    46
    47  // runList drives the `list`/`ls` subcommand: gather the rows behind the Runtime
    48  // seam, then print the pure table to stdout. A gather error goes to stderr (shell
    49  // 230: `>&2`) so it doesn't pollute `pair list | …`. Returns the exit code.
    50  func runList(rt Runtime, stdout, stderr io.Writer) int {
    51    rows, err := rt.ListSessions()
    52    if err != nil {
    53      fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pair: %v\n", err)
    54      return 1
    55    }
    56    fmt.Fprint(stdout, formatListTable(rows))
    57    return 0
    58  }
    59
    60  func buildListRowsForScope(names []string, raw string, index SessionNameIndex, scopeKey string, inferAgent func(string) string, clientCount func(string) int) []ListRow {
    61    rows := make([]ListRow, 0, len(names))
    62    for _, name := range names {
    63      tag := ""
    64      if scopeKey != "" {
    65        entry, ok := index.ownerOf(name)
    66        if !ok || entry.ScopeKey != scopeKey {
    67          continue
    68        }
    69        tag = entry.Tag
    70      }
    71      if tag == "" {
    72        tag = tagFromPublicSessionName(name)
    73      }
    74      row := ListRow{Session: name, Agent: inferAgent(tag), State: SessionDetached}
    75      if _, exited := sessionRowState(raw, name); exited {
    76        row.State = SessionExited
    77      } else if row.Clients = clientCount(name); row.Clients > 0 {
    78        row.State = SessionAttached
    79      }
    80      rows = append(rows, row)
    81    }
    82    return rows
    83  }
    84
    85  func tagFromPublicSessionName(session string) string {
    86    if entry, ok := strings.CutPrefix(session, "pair-"); ok {
    87      return entry
    88    }
    89    return session
    90  }

   206  ### 2026-07-07
   207
   208  Created from a live design discussion. Started from the `pair-dev codex --
   209  --sandbox danger-full-access` crash (codex args leaked onto claude; root-caused
   210  to `DecideLaunch` ignoring the agent + resume-by-name re-inference carrying the
   211  CLI args). Discussion widened to the underlying identity model. Decisions
   212  recorded in Spec: repo as a real scope dimension with repo-local tags (retire the
   213  `pair-<repo>-…` convention), no hash in anything user-facing, and a per-tag
   214  append-only session ledger as source of truth. Not #83 — #83 (per-repo default
   215  agent) layers on top (mode 1.3). Next: decouple + ship the crash guard; claim
   216  this issue and run it through brainstorming into a durable plan.
   217
   218  Claimed issue via `sdlc claim --issue 107`, entered planning via

   220  `workshop/plans/000107-repo-scoped-tags-and-session-ledger-plan.md`.
   221  Architecture decisions in the plan cite ARCH-DRY (centralized scoped identity
   222  and paths), ARCH-PURE (pure model with thin IO seams), and ARCH-PURPOSE (all
   223  consumers and legacy data must migrate, not just the crash guard).
   224
   225  Plan-quality gate returned FAILURE: session-name encoding, same-name repo
   226  mapping, and ambiguous legacy ownership were underspecified. Revised the durable

   228  global `SessionNameIndex` mapping public names back to hidden scope keys, define
   229  live-session mapping order (index → scoped pane metadata → legacy recovery), and

   232  and migration work.

   235  predicate still too loose. Revised the plan with a deterministic session-name
   236  candidate algorithm: full readable name first, then trim the longer repo/tag
   237  component while preserving at least 4 normalized chars each and the numeric
   238  suffix; abort before sidecar writes if no probed candidate passes zellij. Also
   239  made the ambiguous legacy recovery predicate exact:
   240  `tag == DefaultTag(currentRepoRoot)` or `strings.HasPrefix(tag,
   241  DefaultTag(currentRepoRoot)+"-")`.
   242
   243  Implemented the first pure identity slice with TDD: `RepoScope`,

   255  parse/render, latest-entry selection, latest-per-agent selection, and

   259  `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher`.

   262  runtime seam, made `OSRuntime.InferAgent` and the fake runtime prefer latest
   263  ledger entries before derived `agent-<tag>`/config caches, and append a ledger
   264  row during create after final agent args are known. Verified with:
   265  `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher -run

   276  Started Task 6: `HistorySource` now treats its `DataDir` as the current repo
   277  scope directory and lists all tag sidecars there instead of filtering by cwd

   281  'TestHistory|TestBuildPickRows'` and `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher`.
   282

   286  ./cmd/internal/launcher -run

   292  instead of reconstructing `pair-<tag>`, and successful creates append the
   293  registry entry before handoff. Existing tests were updated to seed indexed live
   294  sessions where they mean current-repo scoped sessions. Verified with `go test
   295  ./cmd/internal/launcher`.
   296
   297  Started concrete sidecar scoping: `LaunchNative` now derives the launch data
   298  dir as `<global>/repos/<scope-key>` while `OSRuntime` keeps a separate global
   299  data dir for `session-names.jsonl`. This lets launch sidecars move under the
   300  repo scope without splitting the global public-name registry. Verified with
   301  `GOCACHE=/private/tmp/pair-go-cache go test ./cmd/internal/launcher`.

   314  Debugged a live Alt+n wrong-session recovery: the restarted pane came back with
   315  flat `PAIR_DATA_DIR=/Users/xianxu/.local/share/pair` and resumed the stale
   316  `config-2-codex.json` session (`019f15d3...`) instead of the #107 scoped
   317  session. Root cause was `LaunchNative` dispatching `restart`/`quit` before
   318  constructing the repo-scoped runtime, so `runRestart` inferred the agent from
   319  flat sidecars and wrote a marker that drove the outer restart loop back through
   320  old flat config. Added a regression where flat `agent-work=claude` conflicts
   321  with scoped ledger `work=codex`; `pair restart` now writes `agent=codex`.
   322  Moved lifecycle dispatch and rename/list sidecar commands onto the scoped
   323  runtime/data dir. Verified with `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher -count=1`,
   324  `go test ./...`, and `git diff --check`.
   325
   326  Finished the scoped launcher/consumer migration slice (ARCH-PURPOSE): `pair
   327  list` now filters indexed live rows to the current scope, `pair rename` gates
   328  against scoped public session names from `session-names.jsonl`, and
   329  `LaunchNative` honors an explicit `PAIR_DATA_DIR` override for in-session and
   330  test-harness subcommands while keeping the global data root separate for the
   331  session-name registry. Updated nvim's layout-mode state file to use
   332  `pair_data_dir()` and refreshed the embedded runtime copy, closing the remaining
   333  scoped `PAIR_DATA_DIR` consumer found by the shadow sweep. Verified with
   334  `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher -count=1`, the targeted runtimebundle
   335  embedded-asset tests, `go test ./...`, `git diff --check`, and `bash
   336  tests/pair-rename.sh`.
   337
   338  Implemented conservative legacy flat-sidecar grandfathering (ARCH-PURPOSE +
   339  Root Cause): scoped history now surfaces eligible basename-family flat tags as
   340  `legacy unscoped <tag> (manual import)` rows instead of silently claiming them.
   341  Selecting that row copies missing flat sidecars into the current scoped data dir,
   342  preserves the flat source files, avoids overwriting scoped files, and marks the
   343  launch ledger row with `legacy_import: true`. Flat rows outside the current
   344  repo basename family stay hidden from the current repo picker. Verified with
   345  `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher -run 'TestLegacyImportPlan|TestGrandfather|TestMigrate|TestHistory' -count=1`,
   346  `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher -count=1`, `go test ./...`, and
   347  `git diff --check`.

   361  Final verification exposed live-environment leakage in shell smoke tests rather

codex
```verdict
verdict: REWORK
confidence: high
```

The scoped identity model is substantially in place and the Go suite passes, but the boundary is not ready: several live lifecycle consumers still reconstruct `pair-<tag>` or infer the repo-local tag from the public zellij name. That breaks the issue’s core purpose for prompted custom tags, restart, and in-pane continuation/compaction. The ledger is also not yet the true async session-id source of truth.

1. Strengths

- Pure identity and public-name assignment are centralized and well covered in [session_index.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/session_index.go:94).
- Scoped history plus explicit legacy manual-import rows match the conservative migration shape in [history.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/history.go:61).
- The launcher package has useful acceptance coverage for same-tag multi-repo isolation in [acceptance_test.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/acceptance_test.go:9).
- Atlas documentation was added and linked in [atlas/session-identity.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/atlas/session-identity.md:1).

2. Critical Findings

- [createflow.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/createflow.go:283): prompted custom tags fall back to unscoped `pair-<tag>` and do not append a session-name index row for the actual chosen tag. If bare `pair` suggests `work` but the user types `bugfix`, line 285 uses `pair-bugfix`, and line 395 still only has the pre-prompt `sessionEntry`. Fix by assigning/probing the scoped public name after `chosenTag` is finalized, and append the matching entry for `chosenTag`. ARCH-PURPOSE.
- [restart.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/restart.go:20): `pair restart` derives `tag` by trimming `pair-` from `ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME`. For `pair-work-bugfix`, this records `work-bugfix` instead of repo-local `bugfix`, so restart reads/writes the wrong scoped sidecars. Use `PAIR_TAG` or `SessionNameIndex`/pane metadata, not public-name parsing. ARCH-DRY, ARCH-PURPOSE.
- [compaction.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/compaction.go:22): in-pane `pair continue <slug>` still requires `ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME == "pair-"+PAIR_TAG`, then writes/kills `pair-<tag>` at lines 62-66. Scoped sessions are named `pair-<repo>-<tag>`, so normal compaction refuses; forced compaction targets the wrong session. Compare against the actual public session or index mapping and write markers under that public name. ARCH-PURPOSE.
- [sessionwatch/run.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/sessionwatch/run.go:95): async session-id discovery only rewrites `config-<tag>-<agent>.json`; it never appends the newer ledger row promised by the plan. For codex/agy, the launch ledger row has an empty `session_id`, so the ledger is not the source of truth. Extend the watcher/runtime seam to append a ledger entry when `result.ID` is captured. ARCH-PURPOSE.

3. Important Findings

- [README.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/README.md:229): README still describes detached `pair-*` picker behavior, flat config paths, and `pair-<tag>` attach/resume semantics. This diff adds user-facing scoped names, scoped storage, and ledger behavior; README update appears missing for that surface.

4. Minor Findings

- [decision.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/decision.go:14): comment still says `SessionName` is derived as `pair-<tag>`.
- [list.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/list.go:9): comments still describe `pair-<tag>` rows.

5. Test Coverage Notes

I verified `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher`, `go test ./...`, and `git diff --check`; all passed. Missing coverage should pin the critical cases above: prompted custom tag gets scoped public name + index entry, restart under `pair-<repo>-<tag>` keeps repo-local `PAIR_TAG`, compaction from a scoped session, and sessionwatch appending ledger rows.

6. Architectural Notes

- ARCH-DRY: flagged. Several consumers still parse/reconstruct public session names independently instead of using the scoped identity/index model.
- ARCH-PURE: mostly passes. The pure helpers are clean; the failures are in IO boundary wiring.
- ARCH-PURPOSE: flagged. The single-source scoped model is not yet enforced across all consumers; restart, compaction, prompted create, and sessionwatch remain deferred consumers.

7. Plan Revision Recommendations

Add a `## Revisions` entry noting that the implementation did not complete Task 6/7/8/9 consumer migration despite the issue summary boxes being checked. Specifically call out prompted-create session-name assignment, restart/compaction public-name mapping, and sessionwatch ledger append as remaining boundary blockers.

1. Strengths

Fix by assigning/probing the scoped public name after `chosenTag` is finalized, and append the matching entry for `chosenTag`. ARCH-PURPOSE.

For `pair-work-bugfix`, this records `work-bugfix` instead of repo-local `bugfix`, so restart reads/writes the wrong scoped sidecars. Scoped sessions are named `pair-<repo>-<tag>`, so normal compaction refuses; forced compaction targets the wrong session. Compare against the actual public session or index mapping and write markers under that public name. ARCH-PURPOSE.
- [sessionwatch/run.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/sessionwatch/run.go:95): async session-id discovery only rewrites `config-<tag>-<agent>.json`; it never appends the newer ledger row promised by the plan. For codex/agy, the launch ledger row has an empty `session_id`, so the ledger is not the source of truth. Extend the watcher/runtime seam to append a ledger entry when `result.ID` is captured. 3. Important Findings

- [README.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/README.md:229): README still describes detached `pair-*` picker behavior, flat config paths, and `pair-<tag>` attach/resume semantics. This diff adds user-facing scoped names, scoped storage, and ledger behavior; README update appears missing for that surface.

4. Minor Findings

- [decision.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/decision.go:14): comment still says `SessionName` is derived as `pair-<tag>`.
- [list.go](/Users/xianxu/workspace/pair/cmd/internal/launcher/list.go:9): comments still describe `pair-<tag>` rows.

5. Test Coverage Notes

I verified `go test ./cmd/internal/launcher`, `go test ./...`, and `git diff --check`; all passed. Missing coverage should pin the critical cases above: prompted custom tag gets scoped public name + index entry, restart under `pair-<repo>-<tag>` keeps repo-local `PAIR_TAG`, compaction from a scoped session, and sessionwatch appending ledger rows.

6. Architectural Notes

- ARCH-DRY: flagged. Several consumers still parse/reconstruct public session names independently instead of using the scoped identity/index model.
- ARCH-PURE: mostly passes. The pure helpers are clean; the failures are in IO boundary wiring.
- ARCH-PURPOSE: flagged. The single-source scoped model is not yet enforced across all consumers; restart, compaction, prompted create, and sessionwatch remain deferred consumers.

7. Plan Revision Recommendations

Specifically call out prompted-create session-name assignment, restart/compaction public-name mapping, and sessionwatch ledger append as remaining boundary blockers.
